headlineSummary: Anthropic and OpenAI read as mixed-neutral with a close tie and limited evidence, while Google Gemini is somewhat more positive on UI and workflow quality [E1][E5][E6][E7][E8].

ranking.summary: Anthropic is mixed-neutral with a close tie and limited evidence: praise for Claude and Claude Code is offset by criticism that Claude got stuck and that Opus 4.6 offered little to write home about [E1][E2][E3][E4][E6].

OpenAI is mixed-neutral with limited evidence, as Codex gets some success but is criticized for ugly apps and poor permissions handling, while GPT-3.5 is described as a major capability jump [E5][E8].
Google Gemini trends positive with limited evidence, driven by comments that Gemini 3.1 is pretty good at generating UI polish and that Gemini 3 Pro was at par in a workflow comparison [E6][E7].

evidence.excerpt: I've noticed with the latest models, especially Opus 4.6, some of the resistance to these LLMs is relenting.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says newer models, especially Opus 4.6, are reducing resistance to LLMs, implying improved perceived quality.

evidence.excerpt: > Filip also told me that he asked Claude to continue on the even case after the odd case had been resolved. “But there after a while it seemed to get stuck. In the end, it was not even able to write and run explore programs correctly anymore, very weird. So I stopped the search.”

annotations.rationale: The quoted passage says Claude got stuck and eventually could not correctly write and run programs, which is a direct criticism of performance.

evidence.excerpt: Must be a fun time to work on open problems. I published my graduate research close to a decade ago, often find myself fantasizing about tackling open problems with Claude.

annotations.rationale: The commenter expresses enthusiasm about using Claude for research on open problems, indicating favorable sentiment toward its usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Code is pretty good at Swift + Swift UI.

annotations.rationale: This is a straightforward positive assessment of Claude Code's coding ability for Swift and SwiftUI.

evidence.excerpt: I've have relative success building my own apps for mac using Codex but they're uglier than sin and dont seem to understand well how permissions work.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports some success with Codex but also significant shortcomings in UI quality and permissions handling, yielding mixed sentiment.

evidence.excerpt: Having antigravity is useful because Gemini 3.1 is pretty good at generating UI sugar. Claude 4.6 Opus provides nothing to write home about.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Gemini 3.1 is pretty good at generating UI polish.
The commenter says Claude 4.6 Opus provides nothing to write home about, a direct negative comparison.

evidence.excerpt: In a calibration, it (35b variant) was running locally with Ada NextGen 24GB to do the same things with easy-llm-cli in comparison with gemini-cli + Gemini 3 Pro, they were at par …

annotations.rationale: Saying the compared systems were at par presents Gemini 3 Pro as comparably capable in the tested workflow.

evidence.excerpt: Like how LangChain started on stupid models with small context, creating some crazy architecture around it to bypass their limitations that got completely obliterated when GPT-3.5 was released

annotations.rationale: The commenter says prior workaround-heavy architectures were rendered obsolete by GPT-3.5, implying GPT-3.5 was a major capability improvement.
